mechanism: Landing zones run Azure Container App Jobs on a nightly cron schedule. Job definitions and Docker images are managed centrally; each landing zone runs its own instances.
why: Always-on runtimes pay for hours of idle to run seconds of compute. Container App Jobs cost zero at idle (fits within free tier). Adding a new job type requires no new infrastructure.

related.note: Container App Jobs are the landing-zone-native execution vehicle.
Jobs inherit the landing zone identity — no per-job identity provisioning.
No VNet integration — ARM API is the only reachable surface.
Public pulls from GitHub Packages — no private registry needed without VNet.
Tag remediation runs as a Container App Job — cost-free within free tier.

violations: Automation running on a persistent VM or App Service.
